citrin: noun. A protein associated with type II citrullinemia.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
coir: noun. The fibre obtained from the husk of a coconut, used chiefly in making rope, matting and as a peat substitute.
con: verb. To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.
confit: noun. Any of various kinds of food that have been immersed in a substance for both flavor and preservation.
corf: noun. A large basket, especially as used for coal.
corn: noun. Any cereal plant (or its grain) that is the main crop or staple of a country or region.
cot: noun. A simple bed, especially one for portable or temporary purposes.
croft: noun. An enclosed piece of land, usually small and arable and used for small-scale food production, and often with a dwelling next to it;
fico: noun. a fig; an insignificant trifle
fiction: noun. Literary type using invented or imaginative writing, instead of real facts, usually written as prose.
fin: noun. One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre / maneuver.
fino: noun. The driest and palest type of traditional sherry.
fir: noun. A conifer of the genus Abies.
firn: noun. A type of old snow which has gone through multiple thaw and refreeze cycles and thus is made of numerous small icy grains, though it
font: noun. A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
forint: noun. The basic unit of currency of Hungary; formerly subdivided into 100 fillér.
fort: noun. A fortified defensive structure stationed with troops.
friction: noun. The rubbing of one object or surface against another.
frit: noun. A pre- fused ceramic mixture used to make glass and glazes.
fro: adverb. From; away; back or backward.
front: noun. The foremost side of something or the end that faces the direction it normally moves.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
ionic: adjective. of, relating to, or containing ions
iron: noun. A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a
ironic: adjective. Characterized by or constituting (any kind of) irony.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
nitric: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or containing, nitrogen.
nitro: noun. The univalent NO 2 functional group.
noir: adjective. Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
noritic: adjective. Relating to or composed of norite.
not: adverb. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
orc: noun. Any of several large, ferocious sea creatures, now especially the killer whale.
orcin: noun. The organic compound 3,5-dihydroxytoluene, found in many lichens and synthesizable from toluene.
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
otic: adjective. Of, relating/pertaining to, or located near the ear.
ricin: noun. An extremely toxic lectin extracted from the castor bean.
rif: verb. Alternative letter-case form of RIF.
riot: noun. A tumultuous disturbance of the public peace by a large group of people, often involving violence or damage to property.
roc: noun. An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
roti: noun. A kind of unleavened flatbread commonly consumed in South Asia and the Caribbean.
tic: noun. A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
ton: noun. Any of various units of mass, originally notionally equal to the contents of a tun, particularly: The short ton of 2000 pounds (about
tonic: adjective. Pertaining to tension, especially of the muscles.
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
toric: adjective. Pertaining to or shaped like a torus, or a section of a torus; toroidal.
torii: noun. A traditional Japanese gate at Shinto shrines, symbolically marking the transition from the profane to the sacred.
trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
cro: noun. Synonym of bro (“a male comrade or friend”).
crt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iyojwa'ja Chorote.
cto: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emberá-Catío.
fictor: noun. An artist who models or forms statues and reliefs in any malleable material.
fortin: noun. A small fort; a fortlet.
fri: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Western Frisian.
frot: verb. To rub one's penis against another person's penis for sexual gratification.
ifrit: noun. A kind of djinn mentioned in the Qur'an.
infit: noun. An inlier - sensitive or information - weighted fit
nco: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sibe.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
orf: noun. An exanthemous disease caused by a parapox virus, occurring primarily in sheep and goats but also capable of infecting humans.
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
tfr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teribe.
tinc: noun. The dyeing poison dart frog (Dendrobates tinctorius).
tronc: noun. A monetary pool, in which tips are collected and later shared out between all staff, e.g. in a restaurant.
